method_note: A curated, expanding evidence ledger. Later events can strengthen, complicate, or redirect an earlier claim; they do not prove that an author was simply right or wrong.

generation_method.automation: AI-assisted curation: the seed ledger was assembled during site development with an AI research and coding assistant using the sources linked on each node. It is static data, not the output of a live truth model when this page loads.
generation_method.collection: Candidate nodes were selected because a dated source made a specific claim or documented an event that materially bears on a thread. This is a small editorial seed set, not an index of all media.
generation_method.relationship_assignment: The relationship labels are editorial synthesis. Each label must describe how the later source bears on a material part of the thread; it is not a causal inference, accuracy score, or probability.
generation_method.verification: The verification date records when the linked source and this summary were checked during development. It does not mean the publisher, author, or an independent fact-checker endorsed TriWei's interpretation.
generation_method.filtering: Topic pills run a literal browser-side match against curator-assigned tags and visible thread text. No model classifies or reranks the evidence when a filter is clicked.

relationship_types.label: Early signal
relationship_types.meaning: A paper, report, article, or opinion that framed a consequential possibility before its later significance was clear.
relationship_types.decision_rule: The dated source must make a specific, inspectable claim that defines the thread; vague optimism or generic warnings do not qualify.
relationship_types.not_claim: It does not mean the source was first, unique, correct in every respect, or destined to become important.

relationship_types.label: Strengthens
relationship_types.meaning: A later event or result that is consistent with a material part of the earlier signal.
relationship_types.decision_rule: The later source must document an event or result consistent with a named part of the thread, while preserving differences in scope and context.
relationship_types.not_claim: Consistency is not proof of causation, complete validation, or endorsement of every claim in the earlier source.

relationship_types.label: Complicates
relationship_types.meaning: Evidence that narrows, qualifies, or exposes a constraint in the earlier framing.
relationship_types.decision_rule: The source must identify a material boundary, counterexample, cost, or unresolved condition—not merely express disagreement.
relationship_types.not_claim: A complication does not automatically falsify the thread; it limits what the available evidence can support.

relationship_types.label: Redirects
relationship_types.meaning: A development that moves the story into a materially different technical, commercial, or social path.
relationship_types.decision_rule: The later event must change the mechanism, application, access pattern, policy setting, or affected group enough to alter the thread's direction.
relationship_types.not_claim: A redirection is not necessarily progress or regression, and it does not establish that the early source predicted the new path.

threads.title: Software shifts from explicit rules to trained behavior
threads.dek: A seemingly abstract change in how programs are made became the operating logic of modern AI products.

events.author: Jason Tanz
events.title: Soon We Won’t Program Computers. We’ll Train Them Like Dogs
events.note: Framed machine learning as a shift from hand-authored instructions toward systems whose behavior is trained from data.

events.author: Cade Metz
events.title: 2016: The Year That Deep Learning Took Over the Internet
events.note: Connected neural networks across translation, vision, speech, and the internal organization of major technology companies.

events.author: Vaswani et al.
events.title: Attention Is All You Need
events.note: Introduced a parallelizable architecture that changed what could be trained at scale.

events.author: Steve Nadis
events.title: Chatbot Software Begins to Face Fundamental Limitations
events.note: Connected theoretical work on compositional limits to persistent failures in transformer-based systems.

threads.title: A translation architecture becomes a general-purpose interface
threads.dek: The Transformer began as a machine-translation result; later reporting tracked its spread across language, vision, planning, and multimodal systems.

events.author: Chris Olah and Shan Carter
events.title: Attention and Augmented Recurrent Neural Networks
events.note: Explained attention and differentiable memory before the Transformer paper, and argued that the broader space could extend deep learning’s capabilities.

events.author: Jakob Uszkoreit
events.title: Transformer: A Novel Neural Network Architecture for Language Understanding
events.note: Presented self-attention as particularly suited to language understanding after the initial translation results.

events.author: Brown et al.
events.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
events.note: Showed that a scaled autoregressive Transformer could perform many tasks from prompts without task-specific weight updates.

events.author: Stephen Ornes
events.title: Will Transformers Take Over Artificial Intelligence?
events.note: Before ChatGPT’s public launch, traced the architecture’s move from language into vision, multimodal input, and planning.

events.author: Hoffmann et al.
events.title: An empirical analysis of compute-optimal large language model training
events.note: Chinchilla showed that parameter count alone was a poor guide: data and compute allocation changed the efficient scaling path.

threads.title: Training compute becomes an industrial input
threads.dek: An early quantitative trend connected model progress to custom accelerators, parallelism, capital, power, and supply chains.

events.author: Mike Gualtieri
events.title: AI Deep Learning Workloads Demand A New Approach To Infrastructure
events.note: Argued that serious enterprise deep-learning work required accelerators and new infrastructure rather than conventional CPU-only systems.

events.author: Dario Amodei and Danny Hernandez
events.title: AI and Compute
events.note: Measured a 3.4-month doubling time in the training compute used by headline systems from 2012 to 2018, while warning that algorithmic progress and data also mattered.

events.author: Hoffmann et al.
events.title: Chinchilla revises the efficient scaling recipe
events.note: A smaller model trained on more data beat much larger systems at the same compute budget, redirecting attention from size alone to utilization.

events.author: NVIDIA
events.title: H200 brings 141 GB HBM3e to generative AI
events.note: The product joined compute, memory bandwidth, packaging, interconnect, and power into one system-level constraint set.

threads.title: Game-playing research redirects into scientific discovery
threads.dek: DeepMind’s founding thesis looked speculative; AlphaGo and AlphaFold created a traceable bridge from general learning systems to scientific tools.

events.author: DeepMind founders
events.title: DeepMind starts with an interdisciplinary general-AI thesis
events.note: The lab was founded around combining machine learning, neuroscience, engineering, mathematics, simulation, and computing infrastructure.

events.author: Silver et al.
events.title: Mastering the game of Go with deep neural networks and tree search
events.note: AlphaGo demonstrated that learned representations, search, and reinforcement learning could solve a benchmark long viewed as resistant to machines.

events.author: AlphaFold team
events.title: AlphaFold recognized as a solution to the protein-folding problem
events.note: The research program moved from games into a high-impact scientific prediction problem.

events.author: Dan Falk
events.title: How AI Revolutionized Protein Science, but Didn’t End It
events.note: Documented both AlphaFold’s scientific impact and the experimental, interpretive, and biological work that prediction did not replace.

threads.title: Model capability diffuses outside frontier labs
threads.dek: Release choices, open weights, distillation, and lower-cost APIs repeatedly changed who could reproduce and extend frontier techniques.
threads.status: strengthened; provenance and license remain contested

events.author: OpenAI
events.title: GPT-2: 6-month follow-up
events.note: The staged release made model access itself part of the public story, connecting capability diffusion to safety and governance.

events.author: Meta AI
events.title: Llama 2 is released for research and commercial use
events.note: A major developer distributed weights broadly enough to support a large downstream ecosystem.

events.author: DeepSeek
events.title: DeepSeek-R1 publishes reasoning models and distilled students
events.note: The release explicitly connected a frontier reasoning model to smaller Qwen- and Llama-based distilled checkpoints.

events.author: Moonshot AI
events.title: Kimi K3 announces a frontier-scale open-weight release
events.note: The announcement extended the competitive pattern: proprietary product launch followed by a promised public checkpoint.

threads.title: Scale creates capability—and externalized costs
threads.dek: Early critiques of training data, environmental cost, opacity, and fluent error became operational questions after generative systems reached mass use.

events.author: Emily Bender, Timnit Gebru, Angelina McMillan-Major, Shmargaret Shmitchell
events.title: On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots
events.note: Asked how large was too large and foregrounded environmental cost, undocumented web-scale data, encoded harms, and misleading fluency.

events.author: OpenAI
events.title: ChatGPT launches as a research preview
events.note: Mass adoption turned abstract concerns about confident text generation, data provenance, and downstream use into everyday product questions.

events.author: European Union
events.title: The EU AI Act enters into force
events.note: The debate moved from voluntary practice toward binding, risk-tiered obligations, including rules for general-purpose AI.

events.author: Steve Nadis
events.title: Formal limits sharpen—but do not settle—the capability debate
events.note: Mathematical limits on some compositional tasks qualify both maximal capability claims and dismissals based solely on next-token prediction.

observers.name: Jason Tanz
observers.signal: Saw training systems as a change in the nature of programming, before generative AI made that framing commonplace.

observers.name: Chris Olah & Shan Carter
observers.signal: Explained attention and differentiable memory as a consequential research direction before the Transformer paper.

observers.name: Cade Metz
observers.signal: Connected scattered product wins to an organizational and platform shift across the technology industry.

observers.name: Dario Amodei & Danny Hernandez
observers.signal: Quantified the industrial scaling of training compute while preserving algorithmic and data caveats.

observers.name: Stephen Ornes
observers.signal: Reported the architecture’s convergence across AI domains before ChatGPT’s public release.

observers.name: Bender, Gebru, McMillan-Major & Shmitchell
observers.signal: Connected scale to data documentation, environmental cost, encoded harm, and misleading fluency before mass adoption.
